methods.description: Process for collecting and analyzing sediment matrix samples for microfossils and other specimens, including fish otoliths, barnacles, and sea urchin spines, to understand the paleoenvironment.

steps.description: Removed approximately 225 kg of matrix during the preparation of remains from the site.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment matrix samples from excavation site

steps.description: Stored all the matrix removed during the preparation of the skeleton.
steps.searchable_summary: store collected matrix samples for further analysis

steps.description: Took sediment samples from areas adjacent to the discovery site and from higher and lower levels in the stratigraphic column in 2007.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from various stratigraphic levels

steps.description: Subdivided the matrix into 84 bags and underwent analysis for fish otoliths and microfossils.
steps.searchable_summary: subdivide matrix and analyze for microfossils

steps.description: Photographed, labeled, and reported all barnacles, barnacle scars, and shark teeth in the map of distribution of the whale bones.
steps.searchable_summary: document and map barnacles and shark teeth

steps.description: Submitted all specimens for taxonomic and palaeoecological analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: submit specimens for taxonomic and ecological analysis

steps.description: Detected one large sea urchin spine in the matrix close to the whale bones, mapped, prepared, removed, and deposited it in a dedicated box.
steps.searchable_summary: process and store large sea urchin spine
